text.title: Interview Hot-Take Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Interview Hot-Take Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Hot-take MUST be written within 30 minutes of session end. Captures past the window are flagged 'delayed' and dropped one confidence band.
rule.rationale: Episodic memory of conversation degrades sharply past 30 minutes.

rule.statement: Hot-take is exactly 5 bullets: surprise / contradiction / quote / open question / next-step. Free-form prose is rejected.
rule.rationale: Fixed shape makes diff-able and feeds the downstream card pipeline.

rule.statement: The quote bullet is a verbatim line, with interviewee handle and timestamp. Paraphrases are rejected.
rule.rationale: Paraphrases drift over time; verbatim survives.

rule.statement: The next-step bullet names a single action the interviewer commits to within 48 hours.
rule.rationale: Without named next-step the interview produces no downstream artefact.

rule.statement: Hot-take carries a path/URL to the transcript or audio. Hot-takes without source links are dropped at synthesis.
rule.rationale: Untraceable hot-takes cannot be cross-checked or aggregated.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
